content.full_text:
March 22, 1990

Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
The Traveler's Business Center
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of March 20, 1990 concerning the reporting obligations of the Sacramento Metropolitan Registration Fund (SMRF). SMRF is a recipient committee registered with the Secretary of State's office pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(a). SMRF receives contributions for the exclusive purpose of registering voters within the greater Sacramento metropolitan area.

Expenditures by SMRF are made for the purpose of paying workers who attempt to register Democrats within the geographical area. While the workers clearly identify themselves as part of a Democratic voter registration drive, they do not make reference to, or carry any literature for, specific candidates for state or local office in California.

You advised me that because of the exclusion of non-candidate specific partisan voter registration from the definition of contribution found in FPPC Regulation 18215, SMRF would not be required to file pre-election reports for either the June or November elections this year. SMRF would, however, be required to file semi-annual statements on July 3, 1990 and January 31, 1991.

I would appreciate confirmation of this advice in writing at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

Lance H. Olson

LHO:dr
